proposals.reason: Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/977 published in the Official Journal on 18 May 2026 sets uniform requirements for conformity assessment and notified body operations under MDR. Material regulatory event of the week with direct operational impact on every NB engagement.

card.t: Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/977: Uniform NB Conformity Assessment Rules
card.x: Sets standardised requirements for conformity assessment and notified body operations under MDR: harmonised quotation inputs, mandatory cost breakdowns, and EU-wide maximum timelines. Operational discipline tightens for every MDR/IVDR manufacturer.

proposals.reason: ISO 14155:2026 Edition 4 published on 23 March 2026 with NO transition period. Replaces ISO 14155:2020 immediately for any new clinical investigation. Major impact on clinical evaluation strategy under MDR Annex XIV.

card.x: Fourth edition of the GCP standard for medical device clinical investigations. Introduces mandatory Clinical Events Committees, formal DMC justification, estimand framework (ICH E9 R1 alignment), and advanced statistical methods (new Annex K). No defined transition period: new investigations should reference ISO 14155:2026.

proposals.reason: Health Canada published the final pre-market guidance for Machine Learning-enabled Medical Devices on 1 April 2026. Aligns Canada with FDA-PCCP and MHRA 2026 trajectories; adds SGBA Plus health-equity overlay. Important reference for multi-region AI/ML SaMD strategies.

card.t: Health Canada MLMD Pre-Market Guidance Finalised
card.x: Final pre-market guidance for Machine Learning-enabled Medical Devices. Lifecycle expectations on design, data, training, testing, clinical validation, transparency, post-market monitoring, with explicit SGBA Plus requirements (sex, gender, race, age, pregnancy).

proposals.reason: TGA Australia released finalised AI SaMD regulatory guidance in February 2026. Confirms intended-purpose-driven classification, lifecycle and change-control obligations, and clinical-data primacy over synthetic data. Material for any client with APAC ambition.

card.t: TGA Australia: AI and SaMD Guidance Issued
card.x: Confirms intended-purpose-driven regulation: AI does not automatically raise the device class. Established SaMD requirements apply in full (lifecycle, change control, clinical evidence). Synthetic data may supplement but not replace clinical data; Australian population generalisability must be shown.

proposals.reason: MDCG 2021-24 Rev.1 on classification of medical devices was published on 20 April 2026, alongside the Manual on Borderline and Classification Version 5. Important pre-EUDAMED alignment for manufacturers preparing UDI/Device registrations.

card.x: Updated MDCG guidance on medical device classification, published together with the Manual on Borderline and Classification (Version 5) and EMDN annual revisions (MDCG 2026-1, 2026-2, 2026-3). Operational input for EUDAMED 28 May mandatory registration.

proposals.reason: CNIL 2025 Annual Report published 18 May 2026 highlights AI accompaniment, sanctions trend, and focus on biometric and health data. Sets the data-protection enforcement tone for the next 12 months in France.

card.x: Annual review of CNIL activity in 2025: emphasis on AI accompaniment (AI Act readiness, health-AI sandboxes), sanctions trend, and reinforced control on biometric and health data. Sets the data-protection enforcement priorities for 2026.

proposals.reason: France: two JORF decrees of 21 April 2026 align the Code de la santé publique with MDR/IVDR. National-level transposition adjustments relevant to French market-facing manufacturers.

card.t: France: CSP Decrees Aligning with MDR/IVDR
card.x: Two JORF decrees published 21 April 2026 adapt the Code de la santé publique to MDR (EU 2017/745) and IVDR (EU 2017/746). Affects national procedures, market-surveillance interfaces and ANSM reporting expectations.

proposals.reason: IMDRF 29th Management Committee outcome (Singapore, March 2026) published 7 April 2026. Sets the international regulatory agenda for AI/ML, regulatory reliance, and adverse event terminology.

card.x: Outcome of the 29th Management Committee meeting: international priorities on AI/ML lifecycle, regulatory reliance programs, and harmonised adverse event terminology. Signals continued convergence between FDA, MHRA, Health Canada, TGA and Singapore HSA.
